formulation_source_name: Australian Immunisation Handbook pertussis chapter
formulation_notes: The Australian handbook states that routine acellular vaccines used in the national programme contain three or more purified pertussis components including PRN, so the post-1999 routine programme is curated as PRN-positive.

formulation_source_name: China CDC pertussis vaccine immunoprophylaxis page; Emerging Infectious Diseases review of China vaccine transition
formulation_notes: China CDC states that DTaP was marketed in 2005, incorporated into the National Immunization Program in 2007, and fully replaced DTwP in 2012. The 2005-2011 interval is therefore curated as a mixed transition period with concurrent whole-cell and acellular exposure.

formulation_source_name: Spanish pertactin-deficiency study
formulation_notes: The Spain-specific PRN-deficiency study defines 1998-2005 as the transition period to ACV, with ACV boosters introduced in 1998 while WCV remained in the primary series until exclusive ACV use began in 2006.

formulation_source_name: Finnish epidemiology study documenting the 2005 replacement of whole-cell vaccine by acellular vaccine; Finnish antigen-expression study describing PT/FHA-only followed by PT/FHA/PRN acellular vaccine use
formulation_notes: Finland replaced whole-cell vaccine in 2005 and the Finnish antigen-expression review describes the first acellular interval as PT and FHA only before PRN was added back in 2009, so 2005-2008 is curated as routine aP and PRN-negative.

formulation_source_name: French Bordetella evolution study; French 2024 vaccination calendar; EMA Infanrix Hexa; EMA Hexyon; Merck VAXELIS PI
formulation_notes: Acellular pertussis was introduced in 1998 as an adolescent booster and then generalized to the whole population; French acellular products include both PT/FHA-only and PT/FHA/PRN formulations, so the programme is curated as mixed-brand heterogeneous rather than uniformly PRN-positive.

formulation_source_name: UKHSA vaccine timeline; pertussis immunisation history review
formulation_notes: The UK historical timeline places the primary-schedule acellular switch in 2004. This pre-2004 baseline is truncated to the earliest interpretable historical UK isolates in the current genome panel so the selected-country screen can evaluate a whole-cell epoch against the later acellular programme.

formulation_source_name: Japanese PRN-deficient surveillance study; CDC review of pertactin-deficient expansion
formulation_notes: Japan-specific literature reports that five long-used DTaP brands had heterogeneous antigen compositions and only three contained PRN before the 2012 DTaP-IPV change, so the historical pre-2012 acellular period is curated as mixed rather than uniformly PRN-positive.

formulation_source_name: RIVM programme-change history; RIVM vaccine information leaflets; RIVM what is in a vaccine page; EMA Vaxelis
formulation_notes: RIVM states that whole-cell pertussis vaccine was replaced by acellular pertussis vaccine for children born from 1 January 2005 onward. Current Dutch programme materials list PRN-positive pertussis-containing products such as Vaxelis, so the 2005-2024 routine programme is curated as PRN-positive.

formulation_source_name: Health New Zealand immunisation history; Medsafe INFANRIX HEXA datasheet; Health NZ school booster standards
formulation_notes: Acellular pertussis replaced whole-cell vaccine in August 2000; infant and booster products used in the national schedule are PRN-positive. The selected-country review report extends this row through 2025.

formulation_source_name: CDC ACIP 2018 pertussis recommendations; FDA INFANRIX; Merck VAXELIS PI
formulation_notes: ACIP states DTaP replaced whole-cell doses in 1997; current infant and childhood U.S. products in routine use include PRN-positive DTaP and hexavalent formulations. The selected-country review report extends this sustained acellular PRN-positive background through 2025.
